reason_for_decision: The article clearly explains line of sight as a geometry problem rather than a simple range number, and it gives enough cross-sensor and planning context to pass without revision.
strengths: The article consistently reframes LOS as a real-world geometry issue rather than a simple specification value.
It helps beginners by comparing how visible, thermal, radar, and RF systems experience line-of-sight limits differently.
The practical section turns the concept into planning questions a site team can actually use.
summary_assessment: This is a strong Foundation article: it is readable, technically grounded, and useful enough to pass without revision.
